counselors.bio: Alex specializes in online couples counseling with a focus on communication skills and conflict de-escalation. He has extensive experience working with busy professionals and parents.
Brianna helps couples move from gridlocked conflict to collaborative problem-solving using evidence-based approaches.
David supports blended families in navigating complex roles, loyalty binds, and co-parenting across households.
Samira works with step-parents and biological parents to create clear roles, boundaries, and rituals that support blended family stability.
Michael offers premarital counseling that integrates communication skills, financial alignment, and family-of-origin work.
Elena helps parents manage anger, burnout, and reactivity so they can show up as the calm leaders their families need.
Jeremy supports parents of teens in setting limits, rebuilding trust, and staying connected through conflict.
Nia focuses on sibling relationships and helps families reduce rivalry, aggression, and long-standing resentment.
Omar offers individual and family counseling for general family issues, including mood, behavior, and relationship stress.
Taylor provides online couples counseling focused on rebuilding trust and strengthening emotional connection.
Jordan leads small online groups for couples who want peer support while learning new communication tools.
Lucia supports parents and teens through high-conflict seasons, focusing on safety, respect, and problem-solving skills.

learning_paths.title: Anger Management Basics for Parents
learning_paths.description: A short path that helps parents notice early anger signals, pause before reacting, and repair with kids after blow-ups.

learning_paths.title: 4-Week Couples Communication Reset
learning_paths.description: A structured four-week journey that guides couples through listening skills, conflict scripts, and rebuilding emotional safety.

learning_paths.title: Staying Connected While Parenting Teens
learning_paths.description: Learn how to talk so your teen actually stays in the conversation, even about tough topics like limits, safety, and school.

learning_paths.title: Sibling Conflict Reset for Families
learning_paths.description: Teach your kids to use calm words, take turns, and solve everyday conflicts with minimal referee work from you.

learning_paths.title: Parent Calm-in-the-Moment Toolkit
learning_paths.description: An example custom path combining quick videos, a reflection worksheet, and a practice exercise for in-the-moment anger.

learning_resources.title: 10-Minute Reset: Calming Down Before You Yell
learning_resources.description: A short video that walks parents through a simple breathing and grounding sequence to use in heated moments.

learning_resources.title: Reflecting After a Blow-Up with Your Child
learning_resources.description: A brief article with guiding questions to help parents learn from angry moments without shame.

learning_resources.title: Anger Triggers Log for Parents
learning_resources.description: An exercise to track patterns in what sparks your anger, how your body feels, and what helps you calm down.

learning_resources.title: Weekly 20-Minute Couples Check-In Ritual
learning_resources.description: A therapist-led walkthrough of a simple weekly check-in format to keep communication open.

learning_resources.title: Using "I" Statements Without Sounding Scripted
learning_resources.description: Tips and real-life examples to make assertive communication feel natural.

learning_resources.title: Couples Time-Out & Reconnect Plan
learning_resources.description: A guided exercise to design a shared "time-out" strategy for heated conflicts.

learning_resources.title: Couples Conflict Style Quiz
learning_resources.description: Identify your default conflict style and how it pairs with your partner’s.

learning_resources.title: How to Validate Your Teen Without Agreeing
learning_resources.description: A short demo of validation phrases that keep teens talking, even when you disagree.

learning_resources.title: Collaborative Limit-Setting with Teens
learning_resources.description: Step-by-step guidance for setting rules together instead of power struggles.

learning_resources.title: Sibling Problem-Solving Script
learning_resources.description: A printable script kids can follow to talk through conflicts step by step.

learning_resources.title: Family Meeting Template
learning_resources.description: A structured agenda and roles sheet to help families run calmer weekly meetings.

learning_resources.title: 5-Minute Body Scan for Overwhelmed Parents
learning_resources.description: A brief audio to help parents release tension and reset before reconnecting with kids.

learning_resources.title: Creating New Rituals in Blended Families
learning_resources.description: Ideas for holidays, weekends, and everyday routines that include everyone.

learning_resources.title: Parenting Stress Check-In Quiz
learning_resources.description: A quick self-assessment to gauge your current stress and burnout risk.

learning_resources.title: Using Repair Attempts in the Middle of a Fight
learning_resources.description: Real couples demonstrate phrases that help de-escalate tense conversations.

learning_resources.title: Anger Coping Cards for Parents
learning_resources.description: Printable coping statement cards you can keep in places where you tend to lose your cool.

learning_resources.title: Family Conflict Rules That Kids Can Actually Follow
learning_resources.description: Simple, age-appropriate rules for how everyone argues respectfully.

learning_resources.title: Your Anger Triggers Profile
learning_resources.description: Discover which common parenting triggers set you off the most and what to do about them.

learning_resources.title: Setting Boundaries Without Threats or Bribes
learning_resources.description: A short class on using calm, consistent consequences with teens.

programs.title: Premarital Essentials: Building a Strong Foundation
programs.short_description: A 3-week online premarital counseling series covering communication, money, and family expectations.
programs.long_description: This structured premarital program includes four 60-minute sessions with guided exercises, communication tools, and take-home worksheets.

programs.title: Premarital Deep Dive: Communication & Conflict Mastery
programs.short_description: A 4-week, 6-session online premarital counseling intensive for engaged couples.
programs.long_description: Go beyond the basics with six 75-minute sessions that explore conflict styles, emotional triggers, and long-term vision planning. Includes personalized action plans.

programs.title: Weekend Premarital Retreat Intensive
programs.short_description: An in-person retreat that combines teaching, small groups, and private sessions.
programs.long_description: Two full retreat days plus follow-up online check-ins. Ideal for couples who prefer immersive, face-to-face learning.

programs.title: Couples Communication Foundations (4 Weeks, Online)
programs.short_description: A 4-week online course that teaches the core skills of listening, speaking, and repairing after conflict.
programs.long_description: Each week includes a 60-minute video lesson, downloadable worksheets, and practical at-home exercises. Designed for busy couples who want structured, flexible learning.

programs.title: Intensive 4-Week Couples Communication Lab
programs.short_description: A high-touch 4-week live online lab with small-group coaching and real-time practice.
programs.long_description: Meets twice weekly in small groups. Includes live coaching, breakout exercises, and personalized feedback on your communication patterns.

programs.title: 6-Week Couples Growth Journey
programs.short_description: A slower-paced couples communication program with extra integration weeks.
programs.long_description: Ideal for couples who want more time between sessions. Includes six live 75-minute online sessions and optional office hours.

programs.title: Free Mini-Course: Stop Talking Past Each Other
programs.short_description: A short self-paced mini-course introducing core communication concepts for couples.
programs.long_description: Includes three 20-minute lessons and a downloadable conversation guide. Great as a low-pressure starting point.

programs.title: Core Parenting Skills: Calm, Clear, and Connected
programs.short_description: A 5-week online parenting course focused on connection-based discipline.
programs.long_description: Covers emotion coaching, limit-setting, and collaborative problem-solving through weekly lessons and live Q&As.

programs.title: Parenting Teens: Communication & Boundaries
programs.short_description: A skills-based course for parents navigating the teen years.
programs.long_description: Learn how to balance warmth and limits while keeping communication open during adolescence.

programs.title: Blended Family Dynamics Bootcamp
programs.short_description: A hybrid program combining online teaching with live coaching sessions.
programs.long_description: Designed for stepfamilies navigating loyalty binds, discipline roles, and co-parenting across homes.

programs.title: Anger Management Group for Parents
programs.short_description: A supportive online group focused on managing anger and reactivity at home.
programs.long_description: Includes psychoeducation, skills practice, and group coaching to help parents stay calm in high-stress moments.

programs.title: Self-Paced Anger Management for Caregivers
programs.short_description: A flexible online course for caregivers juggling stress, grief, and irritability.
programs.long_description: Focuses on nervous system regulation, boundaries, and self-compassion practices.

programs.title: Relationship Reset for Stressed Couples
programs.short_description: A general relationships program for couples feeling distant or stuck in negativity.
programs.long_description: Combines self-paced lessons with optional live Q&A sessions on communication, intimacy, and shared meaning.

programs.title: Budget-Friendly 4-Week Couples Communication Course
programs.short_description: A streamlined 4-week curriculum focusing on the most essential tools.
programs.long_description: Includes four recorded sessions and practice prompts. Ideal for couples wanting a lighter, more affordable option.

programs.title: Archived: Couples Communication Legacy Course
programs.short_description: An older version of our core couples communication curriculum.
programs.long_description: This course is no longer actively updated but remains accessible to past participants.

webinars.title: Talking With Teens: Calm Communication Strategies
webinars.short_description: A free online webinar to help parents talk with teens about tough topics without lectures or blow-ups.
webinars.long_description: In this live 75-minute webinar, a teen therapist demonstrates specific phrases, body language tips, and listening skills that keep conversations going. You'll learn how to talk about curfew, school, and social media in ways that build connection instead of power struggles.

webinars.title: Parenting Teens: Setting Limits Without Power Struggles
webinars.short_description: Learn collaborative strategies for setting and enforcing family rules with teens.
webinars.long_description: This webinar covers how to negotiate rules, consequences, and freedoms in a way that respects your teen's growing independence while keeping them safe.

webinars.title: Couples Communication: Repairing After a Fight
webinars.short_description: Discover practical repair phrases and rituals that help you reconnect after conflict.
webinars.long_description: A relationship therapist guides you through what successful couples do differently after arguments, with demonstrations and time for Q&A.

webinars.title: Listening So Your Partner Feels Understood
webinars.short_description: Skill-building for partners who want to interrupt less and truly hear each other.
webinars.long_description: Practice core listening skills, including reflecting, summarizing, and validating, in this interactive workshop for couples.

webinars.title: Staying Calm: Anger Management for Parents
webinars.short_description: Learn fast tools to pause before reacting when your kids push your buttons.
webinars.long_description: This session covers nervous system basics, grounding strategies, and what to do after you lose your cool.

webinars.title: Creating Calming Routines for Busy Families
webinars.short_description: Use small routine shifts to reduce chaos around mornings and bedtimes.
webinars.long_description: We'll walk through real-life examples of routines that work for different ages and family structures.

webinars.title: Blended Families: Roles, Rules, and Respect
webinars.short_description: A practical workshop for step-parents and biological parents navigating new family structures.
webinars.long_description: We cover common pitfalls, how to talk about discipline roles, and how to handle "you're not my real parent" moments.

webinars.title: Live Q&A: Communication Roadblocks in Relationships
webinars.short_description: Ask a licensed therapist your biggest questions about communication in love and family relationships.
webinars.long_description: An open Q&A focused on real scenarios submitted by participants. Includes brief teaching segments and live coaching.

webinars.title: Spotting Mental Health Red Flags in Teens
webinars.short_description: Learn what’s typical teen behavior and what may signal a need for more support.
webinars.long_description: We’ll discuss mood changes, self-harm concerns, and how to talk with your teen about getting help.

webinars.title: Introduction to Connection-Based Parenting
webinars.short_description: An overview of connection-first parenting tools for caregivers of kids ages 3–12.
webinars.long_description: Learn the big picture of connection-based parenting, including emotion coaching, play, and consistent limits.

webinars.title: Anger and Conflict in Couples: Staying on the Same Team
webinars.short_description: For couples who want to handle anger without hurting each other.
webinars.long_description: We explore how anger shows up in relationships, how to spot early warning signs, and tools to protect connection during conflict.

webinars.title: Healthy Boundaries With Family of Origin
webinars.short_description: Learn how to stay connected to extended family while protecting your household’s needs.
webinars.long_description: We’ll discuss guilt, cultural expectations, and how to communicate limits about visits, holidays, and unsolicited advice.

assessments.title: Couples Communication Self-Assessment
assessments.description: Evaluate how you and your partner currently communicate about everyday issues, conflict, and emotional needs.

assessments.title: Parenting Stress Check-In
assessments.description: A brief self-assessment to help you gauge your current level of parenting stress and burnout risk.

assessments.title: Anger Reactions With Your Kids
assessments.description: Identify your most common anger triggers and reactions in parenting situations.

assessments.title: Staying Connected With Your Teen
assessments.description: Explore how often your teen feels heard and understood in everyday conversations.

assessment_questions.question_text: When a difficult topic comes up, how often do you and your partner both feel heard by the end of the conversation?
When you disagree, what best describes how the conversation usually goes?
How comfortable are you bringing up needs or complaints with your partner?
When your partner is upset, how often do you respond in a way that helps them feel calmer or more understood?
How frequently do small misunderstandings turn into bigger conflicts between you and your partner?
How often do you and your partner intentionally set aside time to check in about your relationship (not just logistics)?
Over the past two weeks, how overwhelmed have you felt by parenting responsibilities?
How often do you feel like you are "running on empty" but still pushing yourself for your kids?
How supported do you feel by partners, family, or community in your parenting role?
How often do you take even small moments to rest or care for yourself during a typical week?
When your child ignores a request after you repeat it, what is your most common internal reaction?
In the last two weeks, how often have you raised your voice louder than you intended with your child?
After an angry moment with your child, how often do you come back to repair (apologize, explain, or reconnect)?
How aware are you of the early physical signs that your anger is building (tight chest, clenched jaw, etc.)?
When you try to talk with your teen about their day, how do they usually respond?
How often do your conversations with your teen turn into lectures or arguments?
When a sensitive topic comes up (friends, dating, screens, school), how safe do you think your teen feels being honest with you?
How often do you intentionally spend time with your teen doing something they enjoy, without giving advice or feedback?

assessment_recommended_articles.note: Start here for simple daily habits you can implement this week.
Focus on repair skills if misunderstandings often escalate.
Useful if you recognized criticism, stonewalling, or contempt in your answers.
Stabilizing daily routines can lower overall stress load.
Try these micropractices when you feel close to snapping.
For a fuller anger management plan tailored to parenting.
Covers core skills for staying calm when your kids push your buttons.
Keep this handy for quick resets during the day.
If anger is often about recurring issues, use family meetings to address patterns.
Focus on listening skills to help your teen open up more.
Use this guide when negotiating screen time without constant battles.
Helpful if co-parenting dynamics are affecting your connection with your teen.

assessment_options.option_text: Rarely  usually at least one of us feels misunderstood or shut down.
Sometimes  it depends on the topic and our stress levels.
Often  most of the time we both feel heard by the end.
It escalates quickly  we get defensive, raise voices, or shut down.
We go in circles  we talk but rarely reach a clear understanding.
We usually stay calm and work toward a solution, even if it takes time.
Very uncomfortable  I often avoid bringing things up.
Somewhat comfortable  I bring up some things but hold back others.
Mostly comfortable  I can usually share needs or complaints directly.
Rarely  I tend to fix, minimize, or get defensive instead.
Sometimes  I try to be supportive but it doesnt always land that way.
Often  I slow down, listen, and respond in ways that help them feel calmer.
Very often  small issues frequently blow up into bigger conflicts.
Sometimes  some small misunderstandings escalate, others dont.
Rarely  we usually catch and clear up misunderstandings quickly.
Almost never  we mostly talk about logistics and to-do lists.
Occasionally  we have relationship check-ins once in a while.
Regularly  we set aside intentional time most weeks to talk about us.
